examples.title: Generic Software Process Framework
examples.description: High-level pseudocode showing common software process activities
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SoftwareDevelopmentProcess
BEGIN
 WHILE project_not_complete DO
 // Requirements Engineering
 GatherRequirements()
 AnalyzeRequirements()
 SpecifyRequirements()
 
 // Design
 CreateSystemArchitecture()
 DesignModules()
 CreateDesignDocuments()
 
 // Implementation
 DevelopModules()
 PerformUnitTesting()
 IntegrateModules()
 
 // Validation
 PerformIntegrationTesting()
 PerformSystemTesting()
 PerformAcceptanceTesting()
 
 // Evolution/Deployment
 DeploySystem()
 MaintainSystem()
 
 IF customer_satisfied THEN
 project_complete = TRUE
 ELSE
 CollectFeedback()
 UpdateRequirements()
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This represents a generic iterative software process. Different models apply variations - Waterfall executes each phase once sequentially, while iterative models repeat phases.
examples.timeComplexity: Depends on project size and model used

examples.title: Spiral Model Iteration
examples.description: Pseudocode showing one iteration of the Spiral Model
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SpiralIteration(project_state)
BEGIN
 // Phase 1: Planning
 DefineObjectives()
 IdentifyConstraints()
 IdentifyRisks()
 
 // Phase 2: Risk Analysis and Mitigation
 FOR each risk IN identified_risks DO
 AnalyzeRisk(risk)
 DevelopMitigationStrategy(risk)
 END FOR
 
 // Phase 3: Engineering
 DevelopPrototype()
 IF prototype_approved THEN
 DevelopFinalProduct()
 END IF
 
 // Phase 4: Evaluation
 EvaluateResults()
 PlanNextIteration()
 
 RETURN updated_project_state
END
examples.explanation: Each spiral iteration goes through all four phases. The process continues until the product is complete, with each cycle producing a more complete version.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of iterations

examples.title: Scrum Sprint Cycle
examples.code:
PROCEDURE RunSprint(ProductBacklog)
BEGIN
 // Sprint Planning
 SprintBacklog = SelectItems(ProductBacklog, SprintDuration)
 SprintGoal = DefineGoal(SprintBacklog)
 
 // Sprint Execution (Time-boxed iteration)
 WHILE NOT SprintGoalAchieved AND NOT TimeExpired DO
 // Daily Scrum
 DailyStandup()
 
 // Development Work
 Task = GetNextTask(SprintBacklog)
 Develop(Task)
 Test(Task)
 Integrate(Task)
 
 UpdateSprintBacklog()
 END WHILE
 
 // Sprint Review
 DemonstrateIncrement()
 GatherFeedback()
 UpdateProductBacklog()
 
 // Sprint Retrospective
 ReflectOnSprint()
 IdentifyImprovements()
 ApplyImprovements()
 
 RETURN UpdatedProductBacklog
END
examples.explanation: Scrum uses fixed-length sprints (usually 2-4 weeks). Each sprint produces a potentially shippable product increment and is followed by review and retrospective.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per sprint, O(n) for n sprints
